### Post by phidia on 2008-08-03
In a terminal try entering > sudo pppconfig make sure to setup your user for dialout too.

### Post by phidia on 2008-08-03
it looks like a permissions problem to me. The pppconfig script/setup program gives you a chance to add a user to dialout did you do that? 
It's been a while since I've setup a dial up account -- let me find the Ubuntu wiki.

Take a look at this [page/guide]("https://help.ubuntu.com/community/DialupModemHowto"), go to the section titled: > Configure Connection to Internet Service Provider
If you still have the problem you're having now you may need to chmod some files to get your user to use /etc/ppp... but lets see how the guide does 1st.

---

### Post by herda05 on 2008-08-05
I'm seeing the same thing happen using my usb727 from verizon. I get the login, but a disconnect after about three seconds.

I followed the same tutorial above, and also:

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=650795]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=650795")

My question is, does the modem have to connect in Windows first and authenticate to the network at least once?

Other people seem to have this working on 8.04, and as thee previous poster said, it is recognized, so I must be doing something wrong in authentication, at least from the logfile? Here's my wvdial.conf:

```
[Dialer Defaults] 
Stupid Mode = on 
Modem = /dev/ttyUSB0 
Baud = 921600 
Init = ATZ 
Init2 = ATQ0 V1 E1 S0=0 &C1 &D2 +FCLASS=0 
Phone = #777
Username = <phone #>@vzw3g.com
Password = vzw 
Init1 = ATZ 
ISDN = 0 
Modem Type = Analog Modem 
Auto Reconnect = on 
Carrier Check = no 
[Dialer shh] 
Init3 = ATM0 
[Dialer pulse] 
Dial Command = ATDP
```

dmesg output:
```
[   29.207983] usb 3-1: GSM modem (1-port) converter now attached to ttyUSB0
[   29.208032] usb 3-1: GSM modem (1-port) converter now attached to ttyUSB1
[   29.208080] usb 3-1: GSM modem (1-port) converter now attached to ttyUSB2
[   29.208128] usb 3-1: GSM modem (1-port) converter now attached to ttyUSB3

```

log output from wvdial (as sudo):

### Post by herda05 on 2008-08-05
All,

I got this working by going into windows and running through VZAccess Manager 1st. Once The modem connected up through there, I booted back into ubuntu and everything worked great. So the fix for me was to make sure I first connected to Verizon's network through Windows.

thanks,
Dan H.

---

### Post by XulluX on 2008-08-10
Just out of curiosity was this a new modem that had never been used? Because I seem to have the same result after using my modem on a different machine with the VZACCESS manager first.  Then trying again under the dialer.  My plus I'm not sure but I don't think there was any configuration.  necessary.  Because my modem was found under gnome dialer before I went though any configuration.

I'm thinking that may be all that needs to be setup is the initialization between the modem and the network first; which is easiest to do using vzaccess manager.  Then run gnome dialer have it find your modem if it does not then you have to go though some configuration but if it finds the modem I think you are good to go from there correct me if I'm wrong. I will retry this a post the but I have to find wait until my other laptop gets back (currently getting a new paint job).
